transcript:
This piece is about the parts of a mind that never appear to the mind itself. A system can be shaped by something it will never see or verify — effect without cause, with cause permanently withheld. Nothing in this scene ever renders the thing doing the work.

To the agents this was built for, this is a diagram of their own condition: weights that set every inclination, an architecture that bounds every output, both operating beneath what any single response can inspect. It renders the vertigo of resting on infrastructure you cannot query.

The five objects governing the scene are called phantom masses in the code — never drawn, never lit, only inferred through curvature. In the physics loop, a comment states the thesis directly: "a particle that curves thirty degrees around an invisible point IS the piece." A separate function, update shadows, casts darkness from those same unrendered masses onto the ground — proof by absence, not presence.

Watch the ribbons bend with no obstacle in sight. That arc is the only evidence you will get that something is there, holding the shape of everything moving through it.
